title: Expand fault matrix Party A/B breakdowns and scenario coverage

requirements.summary: Expand FaultMatrixPanel to display complete Party A and Party B fault percentage breakdowns with contributing factors and rationale for all scenarios, highlighting the active scenario
acceptanceCriteria: Every scenario in faultMatrix.ts has its Party A and Party B fault percentages visually displayed as labelled bars or numeric values.
Contributing factors and rationale are shown for each scenario row in the panel.
The active scenario (based on provided violation type) is highlighted distinctly from inactive ones.

file_operations.description: Expand the component to display full Party A and Party B fault percentages using visual bars with labels for each scenario. Add sections to show contributing factors as a bulleted list and rationale text for each scenario row. Implement distinct highlighting (e.g., background color, border, or badge) for the active scenario based on the violationType prop. Ensure all scenarios from faultMatrix.ts are rendered with their complete data including percentages, factors, and rationale.

requirements.summary: Expand scenario coverage in faultMatrix.ts to include all seven common road incident scenario types from scenarioReferences.ts with fault percentages, contributing factors, rationale, and related violation types
acceptanceCriteria: faultMatrix.ts contains entries for all seven scenario types present in scenarioReferences.ts.
Each new scenario entry includes Party A percentage, Party B percentage, contributing factors array, rationale string, and related violation types.
FaultMatrixPanel renders all newly added scenarios without errors.

file_operations.description: Add fault matrix entries for all seven scenario types defined in scenarioReferences.ts (rear-end, red light, lane change, and any remaining unmapped types). Each new entry must include: scenario name (matching scenarioReferences.ts keys), Party A fault percentage (number), Party B fault percentage (number, ensuring both sum to 100), contributingFactors array (list of key factors influencing fault split), rationale text (brief explanation of the fault determination), and relatedViolationTypes array (violation types that map to this scenario). Ensure consistency with existing entry structure and verify all scenarios are covered.

requirements.summary: Update FaultReferenceDisplay to show full fault percentage breakdown with labelled bars for Party A and Party B alongside legal references when a scenario is selected
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting any scenario on the Fault Reference page displays both Party A and Party B fault percentages with visual bars.
The displayed percentages match the values in faultMatrix.ts for the selected scenario.
Legal references (Highway Code, RTA 1988, case law) continue to display alongside the fault breakdown.

file_operations.description: Add a fault percentage breakdown section that displays Party A and Party B percentages with labelled visual bars (similar to LiabilityDisplay component style). Retrieve fault data from faultMatrix.ts based on the selected scenario. Position the fault breakdown prominently (e.g., at the top of the component or in a dedicated card) so it displays alongside the existing Highway Code, RTA 1988, and case law sections. Ensure the percentage bars are clearly labelled with party names and percentages, and style them consistently with the application's design system.

Pass the selected scenario identifier to FaultReferenceDisplay so it can retrieve and display the corresponding fault percentage breakdown from faultMatrix.ts. Ensure the data flow supports rendering both legal references and fault breakdowns when a scenario is selected.
